content: Initial execution pass (cycle 1) was the single largest time block at ~269 minutes. The prompt-writer produced 67 agent prompts but was rejected by the prompt-reviewer on first submission, requiring a full rewrite cycle. Writing 67 agents in one pass is the dominant bottleneck — the writer-reviewer loop's first-attempt rejection multiplied the cost of the largest pass.
sourceEvidence: prompt-reviewer cycle 1 at 02:15 returned 'rejected'; prompt-writer cycle 2 completed at 06:20 (4h05m gap). execution-coordinator cycle 1 completed at 06:44.

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
